content_gaps: Across all top‑10 results the following gaps are consistent: - Interactive risk‑reward / margin calculator – none provide a live tool. - Video walkthroughs of a complete trade lifecycle (order entry → exit → P/L analysis) – only isolated short clips. - AI‑generated trade ideas or market‑sentiment heat‑maps – absent. - SEBI‑compliant, up‑to‑date disclosures (2025‑2026 rule changes on position limits, margin revisions). - Step‑by‑step strategy flowcharts that map decision making from market outlook → contract selection → exit. - Real‑world case studies (e.g., Nifty‑Bank Nifty weekly options around a budget announcement, commodity futures during monsoon). - *Comprehensive coverage of both futures and options in a single, unified guide (most sites focus on one side). - 2026‑specific updates – new weekly expiry contracts, changes to margin‑calc methodology, RBI’s recent crypto‑linked derivative guidelines. - Expert commentary – quotes from SEBI‑registered analysts, brokerage risk‑management heads, or academic researchers. - Rich visual assets – interactive charts, Sankey diagrams for cash‑flow, downloadable PDFs that mirror the web content. - Content freshness – many articles still reference 2022/2023 data; none incorporate 2025‑2026 market statistics (e.g., average daily volume, implied volatility trends).

regulatory_compliance: {"sebi_risk_disclosure":"Trading in futures and options involves high risk and may result in the loss of your entire capital. Retail investors should assess their risk‑appetite, ensure they have sufficient knowledge, and only trade with money they can afford to lose. All positions are subject to SEBI‑mandated position limits and margin requirements as per the latest circulars (effective March 2026).","margin_requirement_note":"As of 31 Mar 2026, equity index futures (Nifty, Bank Nifty) require a minimum initial margin of 10 % of the contract value, with a maintenance margin of 5 %. Commodity futures margins vary by underlying; for MCX Gold futures the initial margin is 12 % and for Crude Oil 15 %. Brokers may impose higher margins based on volatility and client risk profile."}
